body.title: CLOSED: Add read-only palace browser pages to web server
body.result: 8 palace browser routes added: /rack /palace /palace/{path} /decisions /goals /questions /hypotheses /outcomes — commit 441969c, 825 tests pass
body.description:
Akien needs to browse project state (goals, decisions, questions, hypotheses, outcomes, device health) from any browser — including from St. Louis on a Windows box — without terminal access. Add server-side HTML routes to the existing web_server/server.py. No JS build step; plain HTML tables + inline CSS.

Routes to add:
  GET /goals          — list palace.goals.* nodes (G-xxx, status, KRs)
  GET /decisions      — list palace.decisions.* (D-xxx, date, status, spawned tickets)
  GET /questions      — list palace.questions.* (Q-xxx, open/dismissed)
  GET /hypotheses     — list hypotheses from /decided step 2.6 metadata
  GET /outcomes       — list palace.outcomes.* (verdict, decision link)
  GET /health         — all rack devices: last heartbeat, status, OR budget, IMAP bus
  GET /palace         — full adc.palace tree listing with titles
  GET /palace/{path}  — render individual node content

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; reads adc.palace and infra.* via IGOR_HOME_DB_URL; no auth required for read-only
**Scope boundary:** read-only. No edit forms (T-web-palace-edit). No new JS. Server-side HTML only.
**Test plan:** GET /health returns 200 with at least one device row. GET /goals returns 200. GET /palace returns tree listing. Test with IGOR_HOME_DB_URL set and unset (graceful 'DB unavailable' message when absent).
